_note: Seattle neighborhood watch guide for World Cup match days. Update spots and tips as new venues announce World Cup programming.

neighborhoods.name: SoDo

spots.name: The Pyramid Alehouse
spots.tip: Classic SoDo match-day spot. Arrive 2+ hrs before KO or you'll wait.

spots.name: Holy City Brewing
spots.tip: Less touristy than Pyramid. Good rotating taps. Quieter until ~1hr before KO.

spots.name: Georgetown Brewing Taproom
spots.tip: Worth the walk for Seattle's best lager. Starts filling early.

matchDayTips: Street vendors set up on Occidental Ave S 2–3 hrs before kickoff.
Occidental Square sometimes hosts informal fan gatherings.
Rideshare pickup is chaotic post-match — walk a few blocks north.
Food trucks park near 1st Ave S before big matches.

neighborhoods.name: Pioneer Square
neighborhoods.tagline: Historic bars, 10 min from the pitch
neighborhoods.transitToLumen: Walk or 1 stop on Link (Pioneer Square → Stadium)
neighborhoods.vibe: Seattle's oldest neighborhood. Underground bar culture meets World Cup energy.
neighborhoods.bestFor: Pre-game drinking with history. Good group options.
neighborhoods.crowdLevel: High — multiple bars draw simultaneous crowds

spots.name: Central Saloon
spots.tip: Seattle's oldest bar (1892). Intimate and loud on match days.

spots.name: Elysian Bar
spots.tip: Multiple screens, big space, good beer. One of the best watch setups in the area.

spots.name: The Panama Hotel Tea & Coffee
spots.tip: For a calmer pre-game. Historic space, no alcohol.

spots.name: Occidental Park
spots.tip: Cobblestone square. Informal gatherings form naturally. Community watch parties on select days.

matchDayTips: Bars along 1st Ave S fill up fast — aim to arrive 2 hrs before kickoff.
Walking to the stadium is better than waiting for transit post-match.
Post-match crowd spills into the streets — great energy if you're not rushing.

neighborhoods.name: Capitol Hill
neighborhoods.walkToLumen: 35–40 min walk or 15 min by Link
neighborhoods.transitToLumen: Capitol Hill Station → Stadium Station (2 stops, ~12 min)
neighborhoods.vibe: Dense bar scene, soccer culture, diverse crowds. Best post-match neighborhood in Seattle.

spots.name: Rhein Haus
spots.tip: Giant beer hall with bocce courts. Books out fast for big matches — check for reservations.

spots.name: Optimism Brewing
spots.tip: Massive indoor/outdoor space. One of Seattle's best watch venues.

spots.name: Canterbury Ale & Eats
spots.tip: Classic soccer bar. Multiple screens, knows the game.

spots.name: Cal Anderson Park
spots.tip: Informal outdoor hangout on match days. No screens but great city energy.

matchDayTips: Link is the move — drives and rideshare are a nightmare on match days.
Capitol Hill has the best post-match energy in the city.
For early kickoffs, Optimism's outdoor space is worth it.

neighborhoods.name: International District
neighborhoods.tagline: Closest food + culture, 5 min from the pitch
neighborhoods.vibe: Authentic, local, underrated on match days. Best pre-match food near the venue.
neighborhoods.bestFor: Eating before the match. Less chaotic than SoDo bars.

spots.name: Jade Garden
spots.tip: Legendary dim sum. Go for lunch before an afternoon match.

spots.name: Saigon Deli
spots.tip: Cheap, fast, excellent banh mi. Ideal quick pre-match meal.

spots.name: Uwajimaya
spots.tip: Great pre-match food court options. Stock up on snacks.

matchDayTips: Come early to eat — kitchens get slammed once the SoDo crowd arrives.
Little to no World Cup buzz here — it's genuinely local.
Good option if you want food + calm before the storm.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Pike Place
neighborhoods.walkToLumen: 25–30 min walk or 10 min by Link
neighborhoods.transitToLumen: Westlake Station → Stadium Station (3 stops, ~8 min)
neighborhoods.vibe: High tourist energy during World Cup. Hotels, official FIFA spaces, and fan merchandise everywhere.
neighborhoods.bestFor: Arriving from outside Seattle. Hotel base. Pre-match browsing.
neighborhoods.crowdLevel: Very high citywide during tournament weeks

spots.name: Pike Place Market
spots.tip: Best for morning/early afternoon. Head south at least 90 min before kickoff.

spots.name: Pike Brewing
spots.tip: Solid pre-game option if you're starting downtown.

spots.name: Seattle FIFA Fan Festival (Seattle Center)
spots.tip: Free entry. Best for atmosphere and non-ticket-holders.

matchDayTips: Westlake Link station will be packed — budget extra time.
Official fan merchandise and FIFA activations concentrated downtown.
Don't wait until 60 min before kickoff to head to SoDo.

neighborhoods.name: Fremont
neighborhoods.tagline: Local vibe, worth the trip for afternoon matches
neighborhoods.walkToLumen: 40–50 min walk or 30 min by bus
neighborhoods.vibe: Quirky neighborhood energy. More relaxed than Capitol Hill. Ideal for afternoon kickoffs.
neighborhoods.bestFor: Locals who want to avoid downtown chaos.
neighborhoods.crowdLevel: Low to moderate — mostly Seattle regulars

spots.name: Fremont Brewing
spots.tip: One of Seattle's best outdoor beer gardens. Great for afternoon pre-game — leave 60 min before KO.

spots.name: Red Door
spots.tip: Neighborhood pub feel. Usually has screens for big matches.

matchDayTips: Budget 35–40 min travel time to Lumen Field.
Best for afternoon kickoffs when you want a leisurely pre-game.
Uber/Lyft surge pricing will be brutal — take transit.
